content.full_text:
April 3, 1978

Joyce Valdez  
3550 Wilshire Blvd., Suite 1630  
Los Angeles, California 90010

Dear Joyce:

This is to confirm my conversation with you in which I stated the periods during which committees must file late contribution reports.

For committees making late contributions in the primary election held on June 6: May 23 - June 5.

For committees making late contributions in the general election held on November 7: October 24 - November 6.

Please note that if the committee makes a late contribution for an election held on another date, that report will be due within the 14 day period prior to the election.

Sincerely,

Robert M. Stern  
General Counsel

sections.question_synthetic: What are the filing deadlines for late contribution reports for committees making contributions in primary and general elections?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Late contribution reports must be filed during specific periods: May 23-June 5 for the June 6 primary election, October 24-November 6 for the November 7 general election, and within 14 days prior to any other election date.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a confirmation letter without formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections. Q/C content inferred from letter body. Written as follow-up to phone conversation with requester Joyce Valdez.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the filing deadlines for late contribution reports for committees making contributions in primary and general elections?

CONCLUSION: Late contribution reports must be filed during specific periods: May 23-June 5 for the June 6 primary election, October 24-November 6 for the November 7 general election, and within 14 days prior to any other election date.

embedding.summary: FPPC General Counsel Robert M. Stern confirms the filing deadlines for late contribution reports, specifying the periods before the primary and general elections and establishing a 14-day prior-to-election rule for other election dates.
